title: Alopecia areata follow-up
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

treatment_and_management.text: Supports clinician-led assessment, shared priorities, severity tracking, expectation discussion, treatment selection, monitoring, and patient information without authorising autonomous treatment output.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The management context supports recording clinician-completed discussion, questions, an entered plan, and arranged follow-up without generating treatment.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-led assessment, shared priorities, severity tracking, expectation discussion, treatment selection, monitoring, and patient information without authorising autonomous treatment output.

source.title: British Association of Dermatologists living guideline for managing people with alopecia areata 2024

referral.text: Supports full history including site, type, extent, stability, onset, progression, triggers, quality of life, psychological impact, family autoimmune history, photography, severity assessment, and specialist referral criteria.
referral.rationale: The BAD living guideline supports documenting alopecia-areata extent, activity, pattern, symptoms, nail or scalp context, psychosocial impact, and change over time without grading automatically.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports full history including site, type, extent, stability, onset, progression, triggers, quality of life, psychological impact, family autoimmune history, photography, severity assessment, and specialist referral criteria.

source.title: British Association of Dermatologists living guideline for managing people with alopecia areata 2024

exact_section.heading: Section 3.0 recommendations R1–R10 — assessment, severity, impact, information, and referral

sources_used.title: British Association of Dermatologists living guideline for managing people with alopecia areata 2024
sources_used.jurisdiction: United Kingdom / adaptable international specialty guidance

source_limitations: Pattern, scalp and body sites, extent and activity, nail findings, photographs, diagnosis confirmation, comorbidities, psychological impact, prior and current treatment, monitoring, clinician plan, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
